summary: Matched more of the Kraid stage ground object code in src/melee/gr/grkraid.c. The decomp.dev report recorded +2300 matched bytes, 3 new full matches in grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FEA00, and grKraid_801FEE54, plus improvements to grKraid_801FE6D8 and grKraid_801FE818. The useful work was mostly source-expression reshaping, local-vs-global data placement, and signedness/type fixes in gr/types.h.

key_files.role: Core Kraid stage matching work; 168 additions and 154 deletions across state-machine and helper functions.
Changed grKraid ground variable byte fields from u8 to s8 and applied small style cleanups to nearby Old Yoshi cloud structs.

what_changed: grKraid_801FE440 reached 100% according to the bot report. The diff removed the global zero Vec grKr_803B8278 and used a local `Vec pos = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f }`; it also changed `gp->gv.kraid.x4 != 0.0f` to a truthy float check with an inline remark that the explicit comparison caused incorrect ordering.
grKraid_801FE440 also rewrote absolute-value style code into ternaries, kept the rotation update through HSD_JObjAddRotationZ, and continued calling Camera_80030E44 with the local zero vector.

grKraid_801FE6D8 improved from 88.93% to 99.60%. The diff introduced a static `f32 getMapRotation()` helper, replaced an unused int with `f32 rot`, used a ternary absolute value for handpos.x, and rewrote the map rotation speed calculation before clamping to map_rot_spd_min/map_rot_spd_max.
grKraid_801FE818 improved from 90.70% to 91.20%. The visible matching-relevant change was the wait timer expression using `((s32) grKr_804D6A08->kraid_wait_time_add != 0 ? HSD_Randi(...) : 0)`.

grKraid_801FEA00 reached 100%. The switch/state-machine body was reformatted and retained PAD_STACK(0x70), animation completion checks, Ground_801C53EC event calls, hand hit windows, and the same `(int) map->gv.kraid.x4 == 0` checks; the wait timer reset also gained the explicit s32 cast in its random-add condition.

grKraid_801FEE54 reached 100%. Its random position selection was compressed into `HSD_Randi(2) != 0 ? gp->gv.kraid2.x3 : gp->gv.kraid2.x4`, and translate-X calls were kept for both the Kraid JObj and Ground_801C2BA4(1)->hsd_obj.
src/melee/gr/types.h changed `struct grKraid_GroundVars::x0` and `struct grKraid_GroundVars2::x3` from u8 to s8, indicating signed byte behavior mattered for matching.
src/melee/gr/types.h also normalized bitfield spacing in grOldYoshi_Cloud and array spacing in grOldYoshi_Cloud_GroundVars; these look style-only from the shown diff.
smart_moves: The PR targeted small codegen-sensitive expression changes instead of broad rewrites, producing three new exact function matches.
The author left a concrete matching note: `Explicit != 0.0f comparison leads to incorrect ordering`, making the float-comparison tactic reusable for future work.
Replacing a shared const zero Vec with a local initializer avoided the old grKr_803B8278 data symbol and matched the camera-call setup better.
Signedness was fixed at the shared struct level in types.h, not only papered over with local casts in grkraid.c.
Factoring the rotation expression into a private `getMapRotation()` helper made the arithmetic sequence and local variable use easier to control in grKraid_801FE6D8.
decomp_lessons: For Melee stage code, syntactically equivalent float tests are not interchangeable: `if (float_field)` can produce a different compare/branch ordering than `if (float_field != 0.0f)`.
When a state/index byte is used in switch or comparison-heavy code, verify whether the assembly wants signed or unsigned loads; u8-to-s8 header fixes can be decisive.
Local aggregate initialization versus a global const object can affect rodata and stack/local codegen; try a local zero Vec for camera or position calls before introducing a shared const.
Ternary expressions are useful matching tools for absolute values and random-choice branches when handwritten if/else emits the wrong shape.
Cast placement inside ternary conditions can matter even when the field type is already integral, as shown by the `(s32) kraid_wait_time_add != 0` pattern.
The bot report showed grKraid_801FE6D8 and grKraid_801FE818 were improved but not fully matched, so partial-match PRs should preserve clear remaining targets.
naming_conventions: Exported unresolved functions kept the address-suffixed stage naming pattern, such as grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FE6D8, grKraid_801FEA00, and grKraid_801FEE54.
The stage data symbol naming remained `grKr_804D6A08` with a matching typedef `grKr_804D6A08_t`.
Ground variable fields remained offset-style names such as x0, x1, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14; this PR refined types before semantic renaming.
The new file-local helper used lowerCamelCase `getMapRotation()` and was not added to grkraid.h.
New float helper/local declarations used `f32`, while existing decompiled locals still used `float`.
Style normalization used a space after commas in prototypes, bitfields formatted as `xC4_0123 : 4`, and arrays formatted as `cloud[3]`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a truthy float check for `gp->gv.kraid.x4` when explicit `!= 0.0f` changes ordering.
Try ternary absolute values, e.g. `x < 0.0f ? -x : x`, instead of branchy temp mutation when matching float code.
Use explicit signed casts in ternary conditions, e.g. `((s32) grKr_804D6A08->kraid_wait_time_add != 0 ? HSD_Randi(...) : 0)`.
Promote header-level signedness fixes from u8 to s8 for grKraid ground vars when the generated loads/comparisons require signed behavior.
Prefer a local `Vec pos = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f }` over a file-scope const zero vector if the target does not reference rodata.
Small ternary rewrites such as `HSD_Randi(2) != 0 ? gp->gv.kraid2.x3 : gp->gv.kraid2.x4` can close last-byte mismatches.
Retain known stack shims such as PAD_STACK(0x70) in partially decompiled state-machine functions.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report: matched code 56.28% (+0.06%, +2300 bytes). New matches: grKraid_801FE440 97.82% -> 100.00%, grKraid_801FEA00 99.78% -> 100.00%, grKraid_801FEE54 99.85% -> 100.00%. Improvements: grKraid_801FE6D8 88.93% -> 99.60%, grKraid_801FE818 90.70% -> 91.20%.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice; the PR body was null.

follow_up_queries: u8 to s8 grKraid_GroundVars grKraid_GroundVars2 signedness
